meta_title: Impact Windows in Lauderhill, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Lauderhill, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, 170mph wind-rated, A+ BBB. Free estimates—from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Windows in Lauderhill, FL — Built for Broward's Toughest Storms
intro:
Lauderhill sits comfortably in the heart of Broward County, roughly eight miles from the Atlantic coastline, but that inland address has never offered much shelter when South Florida's hurricane season turns serious. From the established subdivisions of Inverrary and Sunrise Golf Village to the family neighborhoods surrounding Lauderhill Mall and the quieter streets of Lauderhill Isles, homeowners here know firsthand that a major storm doesn't lose much of its fury before it reaches central Broward. With 170-mph wind-zone designation and more than nine named storms making their mark since 1990, Lauderhill properties face genuine, recurring risk—and the homes most at risk are often the ones built before modern codes existed.

Approximately 55 percent of Lauderhill's housing stock was constructed before 1994, meaning more than half the homes in ZIP codes like 33311, 33313, 33319, and 33351 were never engineered to meet today's impact-resistance standards. Single-pane aluminum windows, hollow-core sliding glass doors, and unprotected jalousie panels remain common in the area, leaving families and property values vulnerable to wind-driven debris, sudden pressure changes, and storm-surge-driven rain intrusion. With average home values hovering around $280,000, protecting that equity is not a luxury—it's a financial necessity.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped hundreds of Broward County families upgrade their homes with certified, hurricane-rated impact windows that meet and exceed Florida Building Code requirements. Our locally experienced installation teams understand the specific construction styles, permit processes, and insurance expectations that define the Lauderhill market. Whether you're on a quiet cul-de-sac near Central Broward Regional Park or a corner lot visible from the Lauderhill Performing Arts Center corridor, we'll help you choose the right impact window solution—at a price point that works for your family's budget, with financing options starting at just $87 per month.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderhill Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of window companies operating across Broward County, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation on something most competitors simply can't match: 100 percent in-house installation by a team with over 60 years of combined experience. We don't use subcontractors. The crews who show up to your Lauderhill home are Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employees—trained, vetted, and accountable to the same leadership that sold you the job. That single fact eliminates one of the biggest headaches homeowners face in the home improvement industry: the handoff breakdown between a sales team and an outsourced labor crew who've never spoken to each other.

Our experience in central Broward means we're intimately familiar with the building styles common to neighborhoods like Inverrary Country Club estates, the CBS block construction found throughout Lauderhill Isles, and the ranch-style homes that dominate Sunrise Golf Village. Each of these construction profiles presents its own set of rough-opening conditions, framing quirks, and weatherproofing needs. Our installation specialists assess those details before a single window is ordered, ensuring the products we deliver fit correctly the first time. We've completed impact window installations in 500-plus South Florida homes, and that depth of real-world experience shows in every finished project.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is licensed and insured across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, carries an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and works directly with the City of Lauderhill Building Division to pull permits and schedule inspections on your behalf. You never have to chase paperwork or wonder whether your installation is up to code—we handle every administrative step from initial permit application through final inspection sign-off. Our tagline, 'The Kings of Service,' isn't a marketing phrase. It's the standard we hold ourselves to on every job, in every neighborhood, every single day.

section_history.h2: Storm History Proves Why Lauderhill Homes Need Hurricane-Grade Protection
section_history.body:
Lauderhill residents don't need to imagine what a powerful hurricane can do—the community has lived through it multiple times. Hurricane Andrew devastated South Florida in August 1992, and while Broward County sat on the northern edge of Andrew's most catastrophic track, it still endured sustained winds that shattered unrated windows, tore off roofing materials, and exposed countless homes to water intrusion that lingered for months. That storm became a turning point for Florida's building codes, but the homes already standing in Lauderhill were left behind—retrofitting wasn't required, only new construction.

Hurricane Wilma struck in October 2005 as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, and central Broward County bore the brunt of its northeastern eyewall. Wilma's winds tore through Lauderhill neighborhoods with sustained gusts well above 100 mph, and the damage to windows and sliding glass doors in pre-1994 homes was dramatic. Families who had put off upgrades found themselves making emergency insurance claims, dealing with temporary board-up crews, and navigating FEMA assistance lines. Hurricane Irma added another chapter in 2017, reinforcing that no Broward zip code is immune to structural wind damage when a major system tracks up the peninsula.

With nine storms affecting the region since 1990, the historical pattern is clear: Lauderhill is not a once-in-a-generation risk area—it's an active one. Impact windows are the most effective single upgrade a homeowner can make to interrupt that cycle of storm damage, repair costs, and insurance claims.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Lauderhill
section_code.body:
Lauderhill falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), and while the city is not designated as a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ)—a classification reserved for Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the barrier islands—that distinction does not reduce the product or installation standards required here. Lauderhill sits in a 170-mph wind design zone, which means every impact window installed in the city must be tested and certified to resist wind loads consistent with that threshold. Products must carry a Florida Product Approval number, confirming they've passed the rigorous testing protocols mandated by the FBC.

For homeowners in Lauderhill's 33311, 33313, 33319, and 33351 ZIP codes, the permit process is administered by the City of Lauderhill Building Division. All impact window installations require a building permit, and the work must be performed by a state-licensed contractor—not a handyman or unlicensed crew. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles permit applications, engineering documentation, and product approval submissions directly with the Building Division, and we coordinate all required inspections so the process is seamless for you. Attempting to skip permitting not only violates city ordinance but can void your homeowner's insurance coverage and create serious complications when you sell the property.

It's also worth understanding that Florida's energy efficiency standards, governed by ASHRAE and the FEC's residential energy code, require Low-E glass coatings on replacement windows in most applications. Our ESW and CWI impact windows are fully compliant with both the structural and energy efficiency requirements applicable to Lauderhill homes, ensuring your installation passes inspection the first time without costly corrections.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Brands Matched to Lauderhill Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the industry's most respected impact window lines—ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI—and our project consultants help Lauderhill homeowners select the right brand based on their home's architecture, opening sizes, existing framing conditions, and budget. Both product lines are Florida Product Approved, wind-load tested to exceed Broward County's 170-mph requirements, and manufactured with the laminated glass technology that defines true impact resistance.

ESW's impact windows feature multi-chamber PVC and aluminum frame profiles engineered for superior thermal performance and structural rigidity. The laminated glass units—typically a 7/16-inch or 9/16-inch insulating unit with a PVB interlayer—are designed to absorb impact from wind-driven debris without shattering into dangerous shards. Even under the most severe conditions, the glass holds together, maintaining the building envelope and preventing pressure equalization failures that cause roofs to lift. The Low-E coatings on ESW glass are particularly valuable for Lauderhill homes, where year-round heat gain through south- and west-facing windows drives up cooling costs significantly. Homeowners commonly report 20 to 30 percent reductions in monthly energy bills after installation.

CWI impact windows offer an equally robust line of aluminum-framed single-hung, double-hung, casement, and picture window configurations suited to the varied home styles found throughout Lauderhill. CWI's precision-extruded aluminum frames resist warping and corrosion in South Florida's humidity-heavy climate, and their multi-point locking systems provide both security and an airtight seal that blocks noise, insects, and moisture infiltration. Together, ESW and CWI give Premier Impact Windows & Roofing the product flexibility to outfit virtually any Lauderhill home—from a 1960s CBS block ranch in Sunrise Golf Village to a two-story contemporary near Inverrary Country Club—with certified, beautiful, and enduring hurricane protection.

section_cost.h2: Impact Window Costs and Financing Options for Lauderhill Homeowners
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we hear from Lauderhill homeowners is straightforward: what does this actually cost? Impact window pricing varies depending on the number of openings, window sizes, frame material, and glass configuration, but a typical Lauderhill home—a three-bedroom, two-bath CBS block house with eight to fourteen window openings and a standard sliding glass door—generally falls in a range that makes the investment accessible when spread over a financing term. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified buyers, making it possible to upgrade your entire home without depleting your savings.

With Lauderhill's average home value sitting near $280,000, the return on investment for impact windows is well-documented. Industry data consistently shows that impact window upgrades return 70 to 85 percent of their installed cost in home resale value—often more in South Florida markets where buyers actively seek out storm-ready properties. Beyond resale value, the ongoing savings in energy costs, the elimination of hurricane shutter storage and installation labor every storm season, and the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums create a compounding financial benefit year after year.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, transparent written estimates with no hidden fees. Our pricing includes product, labor, permit fees, and inspection coordination—there are no surprise add-ons at the end of the job. We encourage every Lauderhill homeowner to schedule a free in-home consultation so we can assess your specific openings and give you a precise number rather than a ballpark range. Our financing partners work with a wide range of credit profiles, and our team is experienced in helping lower-middle income households find a payment structure that fits within their monthly budget without compromising on product quality.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Lauderhill Homeowners After Impact Window Installation
section_insurance.body:
Installing certified impact windows in Lauderhill is one of the most reliable ways to reduce your annual homeowner's insurance premium in Broward County. Florida's insurance market has tightened significantly in recent years, with carriers reducing coverage, exiting the state, or raising premiums sharply—particularly for homes that still have single-pane or unrated windows. By upgrading to impact-rated windows with a valid Florida Product Approval, homeowners become eligible for wind mitigation credits that can meaningfully reduce the wind portion of their premium, which in Broward County typically represents the largest single component of a homeowner's insurance bill.

The process begins with a wind mitigation inspection, typically conducted by a licensed inspector after your impact windows are installed and permitted. The inspector completes a standardized OIR-B1-1802 form documenting your opening protection status, and that form is submitted to your insurance carrier. Carriers including Citizens Property Insurance Corporation, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Property & Casualty have historically offered significant discounts for homes with full opening protection—meaning all windows, doors, and garage openings are covered by impact-rated products. Lauderhill homeowners with older, pre-1994 homes often see premium reductions of 15 to 30 percent, and in some cases more, depending on their current carrier and coverage structure.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team can help you understand what documentation your specific insurer requires and how to communicate your upgrade to your agent effectively. We've guided hundreds of Broward County homeowners through this process, and we know which carriers are currently most responsive to wind mitigation credits in the Lauderhill market. When you combine insurance savings with energy bill reductions and the elimination of annual shutter costs, most homeowners find that impact windows pay for themselves far sooner than they initially expected.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Lauderhill if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Lauderhill's inland location—about eight miles from the Atlantic—provides no meaningful wind protection during a major hurricane. The city sits in a 170-mph wind design zone, and storms like Wilma in 2005 and Irma in 2017 demonstrated that central Broward County neighborhoods experience severe wind damage. Wind-driven debris is the primary cause of window failure, and that risk is just as real in Inverrary or Sunrise Golf Village as it is in a beachfront community.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Lauderhill home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Pricing depends on the number of openings, sizes, and frame type, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. We provide free in-home estimates so you receive a precise quote for your specific property. Many Lauderhill homeowners find that when insurance savings and energy bill reductions are factored in, the net monthly cost of upgrading is lower than they anticipated.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact windows in Lauderhill, and who handles it?
faqs.a: Yes—a building permit is required for all impact window installations in Lauderhill, administered through the City of Lauderhill Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf, including application submission, Florida Product Approval documentation, and scheduling the required inspections. You don't need to manage any paperwork. Skipping the permit process can void your homeowner's insurance and cause legal complications if you sell the property.

faqs.q: How long does a typical impact window installation take for a Lauderhill home?
faqs.a: Most single-family Lauderhill homes are completed in one to two days of installation once products arrive. We typically schedule jobs within two to four weeks of permit approval, depending on product lead times and our current installation calendar. Our 100 percent in-house crews work efficiently without the scheduling gaps that occur when contractors rely on subcontractors. We keep you informed at every stage so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact windows in Lauderhill?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and current coverage structure, but Broward County homeowners with full opening protection—meaning all windows and doors are impact-rated—commonly see wind mitigation credits that reduce their total premium by 15 to 30 percent or more. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector completes a standard OIR-B1-1802 form that your insurance agent submits to your carrier. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can help you understand the documentation process and what to communicate to your insurer to maximize your discount.

cta_body: Join hundreds of South Florida families who trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing—the Kings of Service—for certified hurricane protection. Contact us today for a no-obligation consultation and discover how affordable impact windows can be for your Lauderhill home.
